duration: Roughly two minutes but heard it off and on for about 30 minutes.
description: I was first alerted to the bird when I heard it calling/singing.  I actually thought it might be a YGVI right from the start as the calls seemed off and unlike anything I had heard from a REVI.  The bird was on the "island" and I was along the south edge so could only hear it.  I was busy doing other stuff and before it finely bugged me enough so I played tape of REVI.  I was going to play YGVI but the Sibley app does not have YGVI.  Either way, the bird immediately flew over to where I was and really started to call.  I initially noticed the bright yellow parts and thought it was too much for REVI but not having any experience with YGVI I looked more at some other features.  The one feature that bugged me the most was the eye line and how it seemed to be rather prominent and maybe too prominent for YGVI.  I noticed that it had a rather large bill as well but to be frank I did not know that was a feature of YGVI.  The other features were an olive-green back, white belly, red eye, white supercilium, grayish face and nape.  The bird was roughly the size of a REVI.
photographer: Photographer: Mark Peterson, Mark Peterson, Mark Peterson, Mark Peterson
